name: Telegram Mini App device hardening and performance QA
description: Harden the TMA client for Telegram WebView behavior across iOS and Android: safe areas, keyboard overlap, theme sync, storage fallbacks, low-end performance, and verification evidence.

planned: Verify keyboard, safe-area, and scroll behavior on real Telegram WebView surfaces, not only normal mobile browsers.
Gate or degrade advanced effects when capability or performance is weak.
Record explicit QA evidence for iOS, Android, theme changes, and unsupported-feature fallbacks.

2026-06-20: Follow up on TMA native-feel performance audit: earlier shell/ready timing, route skeletons, route chunk/data prefetch, cache persistence policy, and reduced auth/storage gating where safe.
implemented: 2026-06-20: Version bump for PR packaging: TMA 1.1.1 -> 1.1.2 and worker 0.1.1 -> 0.1.2.

2026-06-20: TMA native-feel quick wins implemented: Telegram ready signal no longer waits for viewport fullscreen, lazy routes use a layout-shaped skeleton fallback, home and bottom navigation prefetch likely route chunks, and React Query inactive cache retention increased to 10 minutes.
2026-06-11: Add-expense step 2 and step 3 returned to Telegram native BottomButton so the host WebView owns keyboard positioning instead of a custom web sticky action.
2026-06-11: Add-expense BottomButton lifecycle now separates one-time click registration, visual updates, and unmount-only hide cleanup to avoid remounting the native button when form state changes.
2026-06-11: Add-expense step 1 navigates to step 2 with React Router flushSync, and step 2 focuses the amount input immediately from the input ref callback with preventScroll plus autoFocus.
verification: 2026-06-11: Focused add-expense details focus test passed.

2026-06-20: No-code TMA performance audit completed from docs/research/code review. Main suspected delay sources: global AuthBootstrap gate, lazy routes without prefetch, spinner-only fallback, miniApp.ready() coupled to viewport settle/fullscreen, SecureStorage 1500ms timeout, home query fan-out, and missing persistent query/chunk cache strategy. No runtime commands were run because no code was changed.
2026-06-20: Focused TMA telegram-init test passed after updating ready-timing expectations: pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/telegram-init.test.ts --passWithNoTests.
2026-06-20: Oracle review found no blockers or high issues for TMA native-feel quick wins; comment clarity notes were addressed.
2026-06-20: Final ./init.sh completed with Done! after early-ready, route-skeleton, chunk-preload, query-cache, and test updates.
2026-06-20: PR-prep verification passed: jq empty for changed JSON/harness files, git diff --check, final ./init.sh (Done!), and ./init.sh build (OK).

notes: This feature is where the TMA client moves from functionally working to operationally trustworthy.
